function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- First, get all closed cases in the date range
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- Then get owner assignment counts to identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM MAX(newvalue__c)) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Filter to only non-transferred cases (exactly one owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    cc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get the agent with the lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

WITH closed_cases AS (

-- Get all closed cases in the date range with their owner assignment counts
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Find non-transferred cases (exactly 1 owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.ownerid,
    drc.created_ts,
    drc.closed_ts,
    COALESCE(oac.assignment_count, 0) as assignment_count
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  LEFT JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON drc.id = oac.caseid

),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (

  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agent with lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Alternative approach: Find agents who handled >1 case total, then calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as final_ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- All owner assignments for these cases
all_assignments AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c) as ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c), TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c)
),
-- Identify non-transferred cases (single assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.final_ownerid as ownerid,
    drc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    drc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  JOIN all_assignments aa ON drc.id = aa.caseid
  WHERE aa.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Count total cases per agent (including transferred ones)
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases
  FROM all_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agents with >1 total cases, calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
SELECT 
  aa.ownerid,
  acc.total_cases,
  COUNT(ntc.id) as non_transferred_cases,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as min_aht
FROM all_assignments aa
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON aa.ownerid = acc.ownerid
LEFT JOIN non_transferred_cases ntc ON aa.caseid = ntc.id
GROUP BY aa.ownerid, acc.total_cases
HAVING COUNT(ntc.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases,
    SUM(CASE WHEN closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND created_ts IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as closed_cases
  FROM date_range_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  drc.ownerid,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM date_range_cases drc
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON drc.ownerid = acc.ownerid
WHERE drc.closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND drc.created_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY drc.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate::timestamp - createddate::timestamp))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM non_transferred_cases
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY cases_processed DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- First, get all closed cases in the date range
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- Then get owner assignment counts to identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM MAX(newvalue__c)) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Filter to only non-transferred cases (exactly one owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    cc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get the agent with the lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Get all closed cases in the date range with their owner assignment counts
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Find non-transferred cases (exactly 1 owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.ownerid,
    drc.created_ts,
    drc.closed_ts,
    COALESCE(oac.assignment_count, 0) as assignment_count
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  LEFT JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON drc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE COALESCE(oac.assignment_count, 0) = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agent with lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Alternative approach: Find agents who handled >1 case total, then calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as final_ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- All owner assignments for these cases
all_assignments AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c) as ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c), TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c)
),
-- Identify non-transferred cases (single assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.final_ownerid as ownerid,
    drc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    drc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  JOIN all_assignments aa ON drc.id = aa.caseid
  WHERE aa.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Count total cases per agent (including transferred ones)
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases
  FROM all_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agents with >1 total cases, calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
SELECT 
  aa.ownerid,
  acc.total_cases,
  COUNT(ntc.id) as non_transferred_cases,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as min_aht
FROM all_assignments aa
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON aa.ownerid = acc.ownerid
LEFT JOIN non_transferred_cases ntc ON aa.caseid = ntc.id
GROUP BY aa.ownerid, acc.total_cases
HAVING COUNT(ntc.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases,
    SUM(CASE WHEN closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND created_ts IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as closed_cases
  FROM date_range_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  drc.ownerid,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM date_range_cases drc
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON drc.ownerid = acc.ownerid
WHERE drc.closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND drc.created_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY drc.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate::timestamp - createddate::timestamp))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM non_transferred_cases
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY cases_processed DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- First, get all closed cases in the date range
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- Then get owner assignment counts to identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM MAX(newvalue__c)) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Filter to only non-transferred cases (exactly one owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    cc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get the agent with the lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

WITH closed_cases AS (

),

non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,

)

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Alternative approach: Find agents who handled >1 case total, then calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as final_ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- All owner assignments for these cases
all_assignments AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c) as ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c), TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c)
),
-- Identify non-transferred cases (single assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.final_ownerid as ownerid,
    drc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    drc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  JOIN all_assignments aa ON drc.id = aa.caseid
  WHERE aa.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Count total cases per agent (including transferred ones)
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases
  FROM all_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agents with >1 total cases, calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
SELECT 
  aa.ownerid,
  acc.total_cases,
  COUNT(ntc.id) as non_transferred_cases,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as min_aht
FROM all_assignments aa
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON aa.ownerid = acc.ownerid
LEFT JOIN non_transferred_cases ntc ON aa.caseid = ntc.id
GROUP BY aa.ownerid, acc.total_cases
HAVING COUNT(ntc.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases,
    SUM(CASE WHEN closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND created_ts IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as closed_cases
  FROM date_range_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  drc.ownerid,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM date_range_cases drc
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON drc.ownerid = acc.ownerid
WHERE drc.closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND drc.created_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY drc.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

KEY RULES (must follow exactly):
1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

EXAMPLES:
- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "{"collection": "some_collection", "filter": {some_filter}, "projection": {some_projection}, "limit": some_limit}"}} # for MongoDB databases

- list_db:
{"tool": "list_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name"}}

- execute_python:
{"tool": "execute_python", "args": {"code": "import pandas as pd
# rl1 and rl2 are the keys of two JSON-serializable record lists in storage
df1 = pd.DataFrame(rl1)
df2 = pd.DataFrame(rl2)
result = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='id').head(10).to_json(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)"}}

- return_answer:
{"tool": "return_answer", "args": {"answer": "…final plain-text answer…"}}

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate::timestamp - createddate::timestamp))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM non_transferred_cases
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY cases_processed DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- First, get all closed cases in the date range
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- Then get owner assignment counts to identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM MAX(newvalue__c)) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Filter to only non-transferred cases (exactly one owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    cc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get the agent with the lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Alternative approach: Find agents who handled >1 case total, then calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as final_ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- All owner assignments for these cases
all_assignments AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c) as ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c), TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c)
),
-- Identify non-transferred cases (single assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.final_ownerid as ownerid,
    drc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    drc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  JOIN all_assignments aa ON drc.id = aa.caseid
  WHERE aa.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Count total cases per agent (including transferred ones)
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases
  FROM all_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agents with >1 total cases, calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
SELECT 
  aa.ownerid,
  acc.total_cases,
  COUNT(ntc.id) as non_transferred_cases,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as min_aht
FROM all_assignments aa
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON aa.ownerid = acc.ownerid
LEFT JOIN non_transferred_cases ntc ON aa.caseid = ntc.id
GROUP BY aa.ownerid, acc.total_cases
HAVING COUNT(ntc.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case"

    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL

),
agent_case_counts AS (

    COUNT(*) as total_cases,
    SUM(CASE WHEN closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND created_ts IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as closed_cases
  FROM date_range_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  drc.ownerid,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM date_range_cases drc
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON drc.ownerid = acc.ownerid
WHERE drc.closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND drc.created_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY drc.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

  FROM casehistory__c

    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c), TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c)

1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

EXAMPLES:
- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "{"collection": "some_collection", "filter": {some_filter}, "projection": {some_projection}, "limit": some_limit}"}} # for MongoDB databases

- list_db:
{"tool": "list_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name"}}

- execute_python:
{"tool": "execute_python", "args": {"code": "import pandas as pd
# rl1 and rl2 are the keys of two JSON-serializable record lists in storage
df1 = pd.DataFrame(rl1)
df2 = pd.DataFrame(rl2)
result = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='id').head(10).to_json(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)"}}

- return_answer:
{"tool": "return_answer", "args": {"answer": "…final plain-text answer…"}}

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate::timestamp - createddate::timestamp))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM non_transferred_cases
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY cases_processed DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- First, get all closed cases in the date range
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- Then get owner assignment counts to identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM MAX(newvalue__c)) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Filter to only non-transferred cases (exactly one owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    cc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get the agent with the lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

-- Get all closed cases in the date range with their owner assignment counts
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Find non-transferred cases (exactly 1 owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.ownerid,
    drc.created_ts,
    drc.closed_ts,
    COALESCE(oac.assignment_count, 0) as assignment_count
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  LEFT JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON drc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE COALESCE(oac.assignment_count, 0) = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (

  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agent with lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Alternative approach: Find agents who handled >1 case total, then calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as final_ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- All owner assignments for these cases
all_assignments AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c) as ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c), TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM newvalue__c)
),
-- Identify non-transferred cases (single assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    drc.id,
    drc.final_ownerid as ownerid,
    drc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    drc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM date_range_cases drc
  JOIN all_assignments aa ON drc.id = aa.caseid
  WHERE aa.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Count total cases per agent (including transferred ones)
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases
  FROM all_assignments
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get agents with >1 total cases, calculate AHT for their non-transferred cases
SELECT 
  aa.ownerid,
  acc.total_cases,
  COUNT(ntc.id) as non_transferred_cases,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ntc.closed_ts - ntc.created_ts))/3600.0) as min_aht
FROM all_assignments aa
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON aa.ownerid = acc.ownerid
LEFT JOIN non_transferred_cases ntc ON aa.caseid = ntc.id
GROUP BY aa.ownerid, acc.total_cases
HAVING COUNT(ntc.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
agent_case_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as total_cases,
    SUM(CASE WHEN closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND created_ts IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as closed_cases
  FROM date_range_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  drc.ownerid,
  AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM date_range_cases drc
JOIN agent_case_counts acc ON drc.ownerid = acc.ownerid
WHERE drc.closed_ts IS NOT NULL AND drc.created_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY drc.ownerid
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Get all agents who have EVER processed more than one case (including transferred)
WITH multi_case_agents AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid)
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
-- Get date range cases for these agents
date_range_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as final_owner,
    createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) IN (SELECT ownerid FROM multi_case_agents)
),
-- Check owner assignments for these date-range cases
case_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM date_range_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
)
SELECT 
  drc.id,
  drc.final_owner as ownerid,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours,
  ca.assignment_count
FROM date_range_cases drc
LEFT JOIN case_assignment_counts ca ON drc.id = ca.caseid
WHERE ca.assignment_count = 1 OR ca.assignment_count IS NULL
ORDER BY drc.final_owner, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (drc.closed_ts - drc.created_ts))/3600.0
"}

The query result shows only 1 case for an agent in the date range that has exactly 1 owner assignment. This suggests we need to broaden our criteria.

Let me think about this differently. The problem states:
- "In the past four months" (which we're interpreting as 2023-05-01 to 2023-09-02)
- "which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case?"
- Handle time is the duration from case opened to case closed
- Don't compute handle time for cases that have been transferred

The challenge is that in the date range specified, there might not be any agents who processed more than one non-transferred case.

Let me reconsider:
1. First, find ALL agents who processed more than one case (regardless of date range)
2. Then for those agents, look at their cases in the date range
3. Filter to non-transferred cases only
4. Calculate average handle time

But wait, the result shows only 1 agent with 1 case. Let me check if there's something wrong with my logic or if there's just no agent meeting the criteria.

Let me try a broader approach - look at all closed cases and see if we can find any agent handling multiple cases, then filter by date.

- execute_python: run Python code.

- return_answer: finish and return the final answer (plain text).

4. If a tool result is large, the next message will include a preview (first 10000 characters) and the storage entry will be the .json file path (a string) where the full result is stored. To access the full result, your execute_python code must open and read that .json file.

5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed.

So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):

----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate::timestamp - createddate::timestamp))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    caseid__c,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    MAX(newvalue__c) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND caseid__c IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY caseid__c
),
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate,
    cc.closeddate
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid__c
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  COUNT(*) as cases_processed
FROM non_transferred_cases
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY cases_processed DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- First, get all closed cases in the date range
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE createddate >= '2023-05-01' 
    AND createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
-- Then get owner assignment counts to identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM MAX(newvalue__c)) as final_owner
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
),
-- Filter to only non-transferred cases (exactly one owner assignment)
non_transferred_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    cc.id,
    cc.ownerid,
    cc.createddate::timestamp as created_ts,
    cc.closeddate::timestamp as closed_ts
  FROM closed_cases cc
  JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
  WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
-- Calculate agent statistics
agent_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    ownerid,
    COUNT(*) as cases_processed,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_ts - created_ts))/3600.0) as avg_handle_time_hours
  FROM non_transferred_cases
  GROUP BY ownerid
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
-- Get the agent with the lowest average handle time
SELECT 
  ownerid,
  cases_processed,
  avg_handle_time_hours
FROM agent_stats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH closed_cases AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM ownerid) as ownerid,
    createddate,
    closeddate
  FROM "Case" 
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND closeddate != 'None'
),
owner_assignment_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) as caseid,
    COUNT(*) as assignment_count
  FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    AND TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c) IN (SELECT id FROM closed_cases)
  GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '# ' FROM caseid__c)
)
SELECT 
  cc.ownerid,
  cc.id as case_id,
  cc.createddate,
  cc.closeddate,
  oac.assignment_count
FROM closed_cases cc
LEFT JOIN owner_assignment_counts oac ON cc.id = oac.caseid
ORDER BY cc.ownerid, cc.createddate
LIMIT 50
"}
